title: Essay on Shinylive
text:
What is Shinylive?
Shinylive allows Shiny apps to run entirely in a web browser without needing a server.


Difference from Traditional Shiny
Traditional Shiny: - Requires a server - Needs deployment (e.g., shinyapps.io)
Shinylive: - Runs in browser - No server required


Pros of Shinylive

Easy to deploy
No hosting cost
Works offline



Cons of Shinylive

Limited performance
Not suitable for large apps



Conclusion
Shinylive is a lightweight and convenient alternative to traditional Shiny apps, especially for simple projects and learning purposes.

title: About
text:
About This Website
This website was created as part of my data science learning journey using Quarto.
The purpose of this website is to showcase my understanding of: - Data visualization using dashboards - Interactive applications using Shiny - Web publishing using Quarto
Through this project, I learned how to organize content, create interactive visuals, and publish a professional-looking website.
This website demonstrates my ability to communicate data insights effectively to an audience.

title: Essay on Shiny Apps
text:
What is a Shiny App?
Shiny is an R package that allows users to build interactive web applications directly from R. It enables real-time interaction between users and data.


How Shiny Works
Shiny apps have two main components: - UI (User Interface) - Server
The UI controls what users see, while the server processes inputs and generates outputs.


Example Use Case
For example, a business can use a Shiny app to allow managers to explore sales data dynamically by selecting regions or time periods.


Why It Matters
Shiny apps help make data more accessible and interactive, especially for non-technical users.
